closing_result_vs_market.game_total: under consensus by roughly 12.5 to 14.5 points
closing_result_vs_market.NYK_team_total: 109 landed under 110.5, pushed/cleared lower 109.5 depending book

pregame_reference_line.moneyline: NYK favorite around -184 to -225 depending book

phase_1_game_phase_analysis.q1: CLE opened competitively and won Q1 27-24, but this was not built on sustainable shot creation. The Cavs were already leaning into difficult perimeter/guard creation while NYK was generating cleaner rim and assisted looks.
phase_1_game_phase_analysis.q2: NYK flipped control with a 29-22 quarter. The Knicks' main lineup created 62 points across 47 possessions for the game with 63.33% eFG and 64.58% TS, showing the real engine: Towns/Anunoby/Hart/Bridges/Brunson spacing, rim pressure, and assisted shot quality.
phase_1_game_phase_analysis.q3: The decisive separation came in Q3, NYK 32-21. Cleveland's shot profile degraded into 25.7% 3PT shooting for the game while New York continued to score through rim + assisted threes. This was the model's key missed inflection point for CLE team-total under.
phase_1_game_phase_analysis.q4: NYK and CLE tied the 4Q 24-23, but the Knicks' lead state suppressed urgency. NYK landed 109, creating the one-point team-total hook miss despite the side and spread being clean. This validates LEAD_SUPPRESSION_UNDERCUT_ON.

phase_2_pivotal_moments: NYK starting unit dominance: Towns/Anunoby/Hart/Bridges/Brunson played 25:02 together, outscored CLE by +13, produced 62 points on 47 possessions, and owned the highest-signal stretch of the game.
Cleveland creation failure: CLE finished with only 15 assists on 31 made field goals while New York produced 33 assists on 44 makes. The assist gap was the game-script tell.
Cleveland perimeter collapse: CLE went 9/35 from three and only 6/28 on above-the-break threes. That killed the CLE over 106.5 and made their team total structurally fragile.
Josh Hart role displacement: Hart had 16 rebound chances but only 4 rebounds, while his offensive role expanded into volume shooting. This is the exact HART_SHOT_VOLUME_DISPLACEMENT_ON trigger.
KAT rebound funnel: Towns had 13 rebounds on 24 chances with a 56.5% adjusted chance rate, while Hart had only 25.0% raw rebound chance conversion. Rebound access moved to KAT, not Hart.

phase_3_contextual_variables.shot_quality_and_distribution: Both teams posted similar shot-quality baselines around 0.56, but NYK converted the better functional shot diet: 20/27 at the rim (74.07%) and 13/37 from three, while CLE shot only 16/26 at rim and 9/35 from three. CLE's rim-or-3 frequency was high at 76.25%, but quality did not convert into makes.
phase_3_contextual_variables.rebounding: NYK won total rebounds 51-48 and defensive boards 38-32. Cleveland had 16 offensive rebounds, but many were recovery possessions rather than clean scoring fuel. NYK's defensive rebounding against threes was elite at 92.31%, limiting Cleveland long-miss chaos.
phase_3_contextual_variables.fouls_and_whistle: Fouls were balanced: CLE 20, NYK 22. Cleveland got 22 FT points, but it was not enough because their live-ball shot creation remained poor. NYK had only 8 FT points, meaning the Knicks' scoring was field-goal driven and more repeatable than whistle dependent.
phase_3_contextual_variables.turnovers: Turnovers were even at 8-8, but CLE had 5 live-ball turnovers versus NYK's 4. The key was not turnover volume; it was Cleveland's inability to convert possessions efficiently once in the half court.
phase_3_contextual_variables.lineup_identity: NYK's high-minute starting group produced the best repeatable edge. Cleveland's lineups were more fragmented and never found a reliable second-side creation structure.

what_model_got_right: NYK side/spread was stronger than CLE; final margin NYK +16 cleared closing -5.5 comfortably.
Late-lead suppression risk was real; NYK team total stalled at 109 and undercut common 110.5 markets.
KAT rebound environment was stronger than wing rebound environment.
Cleveland offensive fragility was present in the underlying data even before final score confirmation.
what_model_got_wrong: Converted Cleveland team-total over into a bet despite poor creation indicators.
Overweighted Hart minutes and season role instead of live rebound access and shot-volume displacement.
Did not downgrade full-game/NYK team-total overs enough once NYK built control and projected clock bleed.
Failed to separate shot quality from shot conversion sustainability for Cleveland.
phase_4_model_relevance_summary.game_3_preparation_read: For Game 3, NYK side remains structurally stronger, but team-total overs need caution if the market sits around 110.5+ because lead-state suppression can kill the final 2-4 points. Cleveland team-total overs require proof of assisted creation, not just market price. KAT rebound props should be prioritized over Hart ladders unless Hart's shot volume is suppressed early.

karl_anthony_towns.game_3_read: Primary NYK rebound funnel; stronger than Hart despite less traditional wing rebound narrative.

josh_hart.game_3_read: High chances but poor conversion. Needs role/access confirmation before any over.

mitchell_robinson.game_3_read: Low-minute high-impact rebound disruptor; can steal Hart/KAT ceiling in short bursts.

jarrett_allen.game_3_read: CLE's best rebound stability, but team scoring environment remains fragile.

evan_mobley.game_3_read: Volume present, conversion mediocre. Rebound projection should stay below raw chance optimism.

LIVEFLOW_RULE_UPDATE: No 2H or live team total over unless primary creator assists are flowing.
Require at least two reliable scoring lanes before entering live overs.
Avoid team total overs when lead state projects late clock bleed.
Current shot profile must support repeatable points, not just hot shooting.
REBOUND_RULE_UPDATE: Do not play wing rebound overs when the opponent is intentionally using that player as the defensive concession shooter.
If player shot-volume spike emerges early, downgrade rebound projection immediately.
Separate minutes-based rebound projection from role-based rebound access.
Prioritize KAT rebound funnel over Hart ladders when KAT rebound chances exceed 20 and Hart conversion remains below 35%.

game_3_watchlist: Does CLE assist rate recover above 55% of made FGs by halftime?
Does Hart have 5+ FGA or 3+ 3PA early? If yes, suppress rebound overs.
Does KAT clear 10+ rebound chances by halftime? If yes, KAT rebound ladder becomes preferred NYK board angle.
If NYK leads by 12+ entering Q4, avoid NYK team-total overs above 109.5.
CLE TT over only reopens if Harden/Mitchell create paint touches plus kickout rhythm, not just free throws.

next_actions: Patch Hart rebound profile with shot-volume displacement gate.
Patch Cleveland team-total model with creation-fragility gate.
Patch live team total overs with late-lead suppression gate.
Promote KAT rebound funnel as primary NYK rebound pathway entering Game 3.
Limit Game 3 live slate to one confirmed edge unless market gives a clear stale team-total number.
notes: Deep postmortem added for Game 3 preparation. The central lesson is that NYK's side/spread profile was strong, but our portfolio selected fragile overs and a displaced wing rebound ladder. Game 3 prep must shift toward creation-confirmed team totals, KAT-centered rebound mapping, and suppression of team-total overs in controlled NYK lead states. Source support: uploaded scoring, shot distribution, rebounding, fouls, and turnovers layers from 05/21/2026.
